title: ComfyWhitelist
body:
# ComfyWhitelist - A User-Friendly Whitelist Plugin for Minecraft Servers

Looking for a flexible and user-friendly whitelist plugin designed for Minecraft Paper servers? Look no further than **ComfyWhitelist!**

## Why Choose ComfyWhitelist?

ComfyWhitelist is a unique plugin that works with player nicknames, not UUIDs. This approach makes it an excellent choice for both online and offline Minecraft servers.

## Key Features:

- **Nickname-based Whitelisting**: Say goodbye to the complexity of UUIDs and hello to the simplicity of nicknames.
- **API for Plugin Integration**: Easily integrate ComfyWhitelist with your existing plugins.
- **Permissions and Commands**: Manage your server's whitelist with intuitive commands and permissions for enhanced security.
- **Configurable Settings**: Tailor the plugin's behavior to your server's needs, you can customize messages and their color, read more [here](https://github.com/cocahonka/comfy-whitelist#gear-configuration)
- **Broad Version Support**: ComfyWhitelist is designed to support a wide range of Minecraft versions
(**from 1.13 to 1.20**), ensuring usability across different server setups.
- **Multilingual Support**: ComfyWhitelist boasts multi-language support. It caters to a broad array of languages including English (**en**), Russian (**ru**), Ukrainian (**uk**), Belarusian (**be**), German (**de**), Spanish (**es**), French (**fr**), Italian (**it**), Japanese (**ja**), Korean (**ko**), Dutch (**nl**), Portuguese (**pt**), Swedish (**sv**), Turkish (**tr**), Simplified Chinese (**zh**), Komi (**komi**) and LOLCAT (**lolcat**). This feature ensures a comfortable user experience for players around the globe, making your server more inclusive and accessible.

## For Developers

ComfyWhitelist offers a developer-friendly API. Leverage our plugin's functionalities in your projects with ease! More details and usage examples are available on our [GitHub page](https://github.com/cocahonka/comfy-whitelist-api).

## Installation

Installing ComfyWhitelist is a breeze. Just navigate to our GitHub 'Releases' section and choose the version that suits you best: standalone or lightweight. Remember, the lightweight version requires a separate [Kotlin dependency](https://www.spigotmc.org/resources/kotlin-stdlib.80808/).

## First Time Setup

Here are some important steps for when you first download ComfyWhitelist:

1) If you want players to be able to play in offline mode, set `online-mode=false` in the `server.properties` file in your server's main folder.
2) Make sure to disable the vanilla whitelist.
3) Add the players you want on your server using the command `/comfywl add <name>`.
4) Enable ComfyWhitelist with the command `/comfywl on`. This will prevent uninvited players from joining your server.
5) Enjoy your game!

## Commands and permissions
- comfywhitelist.*
- comfywhitelist.comfywhitelist
- comfywhitelist.add
- comfywhitelist.remove
- comfywhitelist.clear
- comfywhitelist.list
- comfywhitelist.on
- comfywhitelist.off
- comfywhitelist.reload
- comfywhitelist.help
- comfywhitelist.status

## Support and Contributions

Got questions or issues? Open an issue on our GitHub repository, and we'll be glad to help. We also welcome contributions to the project. Let's make this plugin better for everyone!

---

Download ComfyWhitelist today, and bring the comfort of nickname-based whitelisting to your Minecraft server!
